title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 8, 2026
headline: The UAE's published Developing-band score did not price its documented, ongoing arming of the RSF in Sudan's genocide — a new ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY ruling pulls INT, ACC, SYS, and EMP dimensions down, crossing UAE from Developing to Critical (23.4 → 18.4, −5.0); the first band crossing in several cycles. Sudan's absolute floor is reinforced, not supplanted: external sponsorship scored once under the sponsor, never double-counted against the in-territory perpetrator.
summary: One scored proposal this cycle — the first band crossing in several cycles. The UAE's published 23.4 priced it as an ordinary mid-band autocracy with constrained civic space and migrant-labor inequity. It did not price the documented, ongoing UAE financing, arming, and mercenary-supply of the RSF in Sudan, a force the UN Fact-Finding Mission (Feb 19, 2026) found bears the hallmarks of genocide. The Conflict Insights Group (June 2026) confirmed UAE support enabled the El Fasher mass atrocities of October 2025; Amnesty documented UAE re-exporting Chinese weapons to the RSF in breach of the UN embargo via Libya, Chad, and Uganda; Sudan filed at the ICJ (March 2025) alleging UAE complicity in genocide; Refugees International issued a new call for action June 8. The new ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY ruling (first application) pulls INT 1.5 → 1.2, ACC 1.5 → 1.2, SYS 2.0 → 1.5, and EMP 2.0 → 1.5 while preserving BND 3.0 — domestic scope capacity is unchanged. The UAE is a complicit external sponsor, not an in-territory perpetrator; the ICJ case is unadjudicated, which caps the magnitude and precludes a floor designation. Result: 23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0, medium-high confidence. Sudan's 0.0 floor is reinforced by the same evidence — external-sponsorship scored under UAE, never against Sudan's floor. Russia confirmed at 0.0 (June 1–2 largest 2026 bombardment: 656 drones and 73 missiles, 22 civilians killed including children; Putin rejected talks June 6). Israel confirmed at 0.0 (970+ killed since October 2025 ceasefire; June 6 tent-camp strike; West Bank operations; OHCHR ethnic-cleansing concerns). Iran confirmed at floor-adjacent 2.5 (784 executions YTD, 126th week of prison-execution protests). DRC confirmed at near-floor 2.3 (Ebola PHEIC, 515 cases, 91 deaths, no approved vaccine for the Bundibugyo strain). Microsoft confirmed at 65.3 with positive-watch: the June 8 published Azure-Israel inquiry is the compelled remediation already assessed June 7 — COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION, sub-threshold. Alphabet confirmed at 40.0 at the exact Developing/Functional boundary: no entity-specific evidence in the lookback. Oracle boundary-watch carries forward at 20.6 with the June 15 WARN sign-or-forfeit deadline seven days out. Five rotation confirms: Luxembourg 81.3, Liechtenstein 83.0, San Jose 48.4, Pittsburgh 45.3, Unitree Robotics 35.9.

topSignals.title: United Arab Emirates 23.4 → 18.4 — ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY: Band Crossing Developing → Critical Proposed
topSignals.description: The UAE's published 23.4 composite priced it as an ordinary mid-band autocracy with constrained civic space, migrant-labor inequity, and weak accountability institutions. It did not price the documented, ongoing UAE financing, arming, and mercenary-supply of the Rapid Support Forces in Sudan — a force the UN Fact-Finding Mission (Feb 19, 2026) found bears the hallmarks of genocide. New evidence this cycle: the Conflict Insights Group (June 2026) confirms UAE weapons and mercenaries enabled the El Fasher mass atrocities of October 2025; Amnesty documents UAE re-exporting Chinese weaponry to the RSF in breach of the UN arms embargo via Libya, Chad, and Uganda; UK-origin equipment is documented reaching the RSF via UAE-connected supply chains; Sudan's ICJ application (March 2025) alleges UAE complicity in genocide through extensive financial, political, and military RSF support. The ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY ruling (new this cycle) pulls the integrity-of-care dimensions: INT 1.5 → 1.2, ACC 1.5 → 1.2, SYS 2.0 → 1.5, EMP 2.0 → 1.5. BND 3.0 is preserved — UAE's domestic scope and service-delivery capacity are distinct from its external-conduct profile. The UAE is a complicit external sponsor, not an in-territory perpetrator; the ICJ case is unadjudicated, capping the magnitude and precluding a floor designation. Composite: 23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0, medium-high confidence. Sudan's 0.0 floor is reinforced by the same evidence and scored separately — no double-counting.

topSignals.title: Sudan 0.0 — Floor Reinforced by UAE External-State-Sponsorship Documentation; El Fasher Evidence Chain Completed
topSignals.description: The June 8 evidence cycle completes the external-state-sponsorship chain for Sudan's active-perpetration floor: UAE weapons, mercenaries, and financing to the RSF — now confirmed by Conflict Insights Group, Amnesty, and the Sudan ICJ filing — constitute the external-enablement dimension of Sudan's 0.0 designation. The floor cannot move lower, but the significance is evidentiary: a state actor is documented as actively enabling a force the UN has confirmed committed acts bearing the hallmarks of genocide. Sudan's 0.0 encodes an estimated 400,000 dead, 13.6 million displaced, and 19.1 million food-insecure across the ongoing RSF conflict. External-state-sponsorship evidence scored under UAE; Sudan floor carries forward as in-territory perpetration.

topSignals.title: Microsoft 65.3 — COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION Confirmed; June 8 Azure-Israel Publication Is the Priced Remediation
topSignals.description: Microsoft published the findings of its Azure-Israel internal investigation on June 8: Unit 8200 used Azure to collect, replay, and analyse content of millions of Palestinian phone calls daily (a ToS violation); that access was terminated and a Human Rights Review Board announced. The head of Microsoft Israel resigned May 31. This publication is the compelled remediation assessed June 7 — triggered directly by the August 2025 Guardian/+972/Local Call exposé, with the Covington review scope-limited by design (never examined the military data) and the board a prospective commitment with no observable outputs yet. Under COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION, this maps to AB2 anchor 3 (course correction under pressure), not anchor 4–5 self-initiation. Maximum defensible move ACC 3.8 → 4.0 / INT 3.0 → 3.1 = +1.3 (66.6), sub-threshold. AB5 reparation to surveilled Palestinians absent. Confirmed at 65.3 Established with positive-watch active.

topSignals.title: Russia 0.0 — 2026's Largest Bombardment Confirmed; 656 Drones and 73 Missiles, 22 Civilians Killed
topSignals.description: The June 1–2 Russian bombardment of Ukraine — 656 drones and 73 missiles, the largest single combined attack of 2026 — killed 22 civilians including children. Putin rejected ceasefire talks June 6. UN-verified civilian casualties since February 2022 now exceed 60,659. Russia's 0.0 active-perpetration floor holds; the June 8 bombardment pattern reinforces without creating further scoring movement. Confirmed at 0.0.

topSignals.title: Israel 0.0 — 970+ Killed Since October 2025 Ceasefire; June 6 Tent-Camp Strike; OHCHR Ethnic-Cleansing Concerns
topSignals.description: Post-ceasefire (October 2025): 970+ Palestinians killed, 3,076+ ceasefire violations, aid inflow at 36% of allocation, and 37+ aid groups banned from operating. A June 6 strike on a tent camp was documented. OHCHR has raised ethnic-cleansing concerns regarding West Bank operations, with 36,000+ forcibly displaced. ICC warrants for Netanyahu and Gallant remain active. The Microsoft Azure/Unit-8200 remediation is scored under Microsoft, not as a modification to this sovereign floor. Israel confirmed at 0.0.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 — 784 Executions YTD; 126th Week of Prison Execution Protests
topSignals.description: Iran's 'No to Executions Tuesdays' campaign entered its 126th consecutive week across 56 facilities. The 784 executions recorded through June 8, 2026 — including political prisoners and capital sentences for drug offenses below the international threshold — represent the highest annual pace in decades. Secret political executions were documented June 1. The single-dominant-extremity hold at near-floor 2.5 is maintained: the multi-category absolute-0.0 threshold is not met absent a second dominant harm category. Confirmed at 2.5.

topSignals.title: Democratic Republic of Congo 2.3 — Ebola PHEIC at 515 Cases / 91 Deaths; No Approved Vaccine for Bundibugyo Strain
topSignals.description: The DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak stands at 515 confirmed cases and 91 deaths under an active WHO PHEIC, with no approved vaccine for the Bundibugyo variant. Contact tracing covers an estimated 20% of known transmission chains across 17 of 36 Ituri health zones. The concurrent humanitarian burden includes 26.5 million food-insecure and the ongoing M23/AFC armed conflict in eastern DRC. The outbreak is an exogenous epidemiological shock revealing already-priced state-response incapacity; documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response is the conversion trigger from the current near-floor 2.3 hold. Confirmed at 2.3.

topSignals.title: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Act June 15 Sign-or-Forfeit Window Seven Days Out; Boundary at 0.6 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window for approximately 30,000 affected workers closes June 15 — seven days from this cycle. Oracle satisfied the WARN Act's 60-day notice requirement by placing workers on paid administrative leave, absorbing WARN pay into severance and leaving workers excluded from the program with zero net payout. Affected groups include cancer patients, pregnant employees, H-1B visa holders at deportation risk, veterans, and workers within RSU vesting milestones. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is active in the Washington state class action and Missouri state investigation. Oracle confirmed at 20.6 boundary-watch; adverse adjudication would likely push the composite into Critical from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.

topSignals.title: Alphabet/Google 40.0 — COMPELLED-REMEDY Hold at Exact Developing/Functional Boundary
topSignals.description: Alphabet confirmed at 40.0, sitting at the exact Developing/Functional band boundary (40/41). The June 4 Norway sovereign wealth fund governance proposal on Alphabet's Project Nimbus oversight is institutional-investor pressure, not an executed action or new harm. No entity-specific adverse evidence surfaced in the lookback window. The compelled-remedy hold from the June 3 CMA/Apple Books ruling carries forward. Resolution turns on whether Alphabet's governance reform on military-cloud human-rights review constitutes a verified executed action with observable outcomes — that conversion has not occurred this cycle. Confirmed at 40.0.

topSignals.title: UnitedHealth Group 10.2 — DOJ Grand-Jury Subpoena Added; Four Converging Sovereign-Enforcement Actions
topSignals.description: UnitedHealth Group's enforcement density reached its highest concentration in the current cycle: DOJ Massachusetts criminal and civil fraud probe, AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit (plus MultiPlan), Lifepoint June 2 federal complaint, and DOJ Antitrust MultiPlan grand-jury subpoena. The four actions converge on the same upcoding-and-claims-denial pattern. Pre-adjudication discipline holds: no single action has reached merits determination; collectively they corroborate the 10.2 near-floor profile without crossing into individually scored territory. Confirmed at 10.2; merits adjudication or settlement on any of the four actions is the leading corporate scoring trigger of the cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: ICJ ruling in Sudan's case alleging UAE complicity in genocide (filed March 2025); verified cessation of RSF arms transfers constitutes the primary reversal signal. Documented direct UAE-force participation in Sudan would move toward a floor assessment.
boundaryWatch.note: UAE sits 3.4 points above the Developing/Critical boundary on its published score; the proposed composite of 18.4 places it 1.6 points above the Critical-band floor. The ICJ case is unadjudicated — a key constraint on the magnitude. The complicity evidence pattern is documented across four independent sources from Feb–June 2026.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day window closes June 15. Washington class action and Missouri state investigation on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory are the active adjudication channels. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the avoidance theory constitutes an immediate scoring trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 0.6 points above Critical — the narrowest active boundary margin. The June 15 deadline is the most proximate hard trigger date across all assessed entities. Zero-net-payout structure for newer and excluded workers deepens the EMP dimension record each cycle the window remains open.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Verified executed governance reform on military-cloud human rights review that produces observable outcomes — a documented decision blocking or exiting a harmful contract — constitutes a scorable upward signal from the exact band boundary. New entity-specific adverse evidence on Project Nimbus would be an immediate downward trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Alphabet sits at the exact 40/41 Developing/Functional boundary — zero-point margin. Norway sovereign wealth fund governance proposal (June 4) is institutional-investor pressure; EFF's June 8 call for Google to follow Microsoft's COMPELLED-REMEDY model places the Project Nimbus contract under the same scrutiny framework.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal. Further military deployments with documented casualties under the signed law reinforce the pending 18.4 Critical composite.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia now at its applied 18.4 score following the June 9 commit. The pending 06-06 proposal documented the CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION pattern; the same-regime-continuation evidence of this cycle confirms without re-proposing. Bolivia sits 1.6 points above the Critical-band floor.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions is the primary reassessment signal. Reinstatement of the rescinded June 4 ICE post-release death-reporting requirement would be the first upward-direction indicator within the applied 17.5 Critical baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: United States now at its applied 17.5 score following the June 9 commit. The June 4 ICE death-reporting rescission deepens the ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor evidence — 18 in-custody deaths YTD at approximately one every six days, the highest rate in more than two decades — without altering the magnitude. Confirmed as same-regime continuation.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit; Phase-2 MDL July 31 deadline is the next structural trigger. Military-contractor federal immunity defense ruling also operative.
boundaryWatch.note: 3M at 0.3 points above Critical — tied with Uganda for the narrowest active country/corporate boundary margin. The Phase-2 MDL deadline July 31 is the nearest hard structural trigger.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of humanitarian access, a state-attributable mass-casualty event, or IPC Phase 5 famine-area confirmation converts the carry-forward to a scorable downgrade.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 1.9 points above Critical. Approximately 34.7 million lean-season food-insecure and 3.5 million IDPs reflect non-state armed group and structural-fragility drivers priced at the 21.9 baseline. West and Central Africa lean season active through August with 55 million food-insecure across the region.

recentAssessments.whyHeadline: Rotation confirm at 81.3 Exemplary; no acute event; known top-band cohort baseline anomaly noted.
Rotation confirm at 83.0 Exemplary; high-capacity micro-state; EQU 3.5 residual gap noted.

emergingRisks.risk: UAE continues to accumulate documented complicity evidence in Sudan's RSF genocide; the ICJ case (filed March 2025) is the next adjudication milestone, with four independent source chains now corroborating the pattern.
emergingRisks.detail: The proposed UAE 18.4 Critical composite rests on four independent source chains (UN FFM Feb 2026, Conflict Insights Group June 2026, Amnesty International, Sudan ICJ filing March 2025). Each additional cycle without RSF arms-transfer cessation deepens the evidence record. Verified direct UAE-force participation in Sudan would move toward a floor assessment; ICJ adjudication finding UAE liable would remove the magnitude cap currently imposed by the pending-adjudication constraint.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closes June 15 — the most proximate hard trigger date across all assessed entities; adverse adjudication on the remote-reclassification avoidance theory would cross from 0.6 points above Critical into Critical.
emergingRisks.detail: Approximately 30,000 workers remain in Oracle's WARN window. Washington class action and Missouri state investigation on the remote-reclassification avoidance theory are both active. The zero-net-payout structure for newer and excluded workers deepens the EMP record. Cycle 12 of the Oracle boundary watch.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2 — 55 days from this cycle — with no assessed AI lab having published compliance documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: Non-compliance fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. The Great American AI Act (June 4–5, bipartisan) adds mandatory twice-yearly audits and $1M/day penalties. Both instruments bear on the same compliance-documentation gap. The August 2 EU AI Act date is the nearer of the two; no assessed lab has published the required transparency documentation.

emergingRisks.risk: Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in DRC continues to accelerate; the absence of an approved vaccine for the Bundibugyo strain and contact-tracing at 20% of known chains represent an unresolved systemic gap for the near-floor 2.3 composite.
emergingRisks.detail: DRC at near-floor 2.3 and Uganda at 0.3 points above Critical both rest on the absence of documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response. The 17-health-zone spread across Ituri, the lack of an approved Bundibugyo vaccine, and the 20% contact-tracing coverage represent a qualitative escalation in outbreak risk. Documented state obstruction of WHO access constitutes the conversion trigger from the current hold.

emergingRisks.risk: ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY (new ruling, first application to UAE) creates a precedent for other external-state sponsors of documented atrocity — the methodology now has a scored category between the ordinary-autocracy baseline and the in-territory active-perpetration floor.
emergingRisks.detail: The UAE case is the methodology's first application of external-state-sponsorship-of-genocide as a scored downgrade category. Future cycles may encounter other candidates (third-party state arms suppliers to documented atrocity actors). The scoring discipline — complicity once under the sponsor, no double-count against the in-territory perpetrator — is now anchored in the first live case.

emergingRisks.risk: AZ AG cartel suit naming eight health insurers and MultiPlan carries sector-wide ACC and EQU exposure; if the cartel designation is adjudicated, multiple Fortune 500 health insurers face simultaneous scoring events.
emergingRisks.detail: The AZ AG suit names UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, Humana, BCBS Arizona, Centene, Molina Healthcare, and Elevance Health alongside MultiPlan. Adjudicated cartel designation would extend ACC and EQU scoring exposure across the Fortune 500 health-insurer cluster — a sector-wide repricing event rather than an entity-specific one.

emergingRisks.risk: Fortune 500 DEI disclosure decline (65% year-over-year) continues to weaken the EQU and EMP transparency environment; EEOC Title VII warning on DEI-elimination programs introduces a new enforcement-density signal.
emergingRisks.detail: The 65% DEI disclosure decline is a structural weakening of the ACC-transparency environment relevant to EQU and EMP scoring. The EEOC Title VII warning on Fortune 500 DEI-elimination programs introduces a potential enforcement channel. Neither pattern has crossed an individual entity's proposal threshold in this cycle; both are monitored as index-wide drift signals.

sectorAlerts.headline: UAE's published Developing-band score corrected for un-priced genocide complicity; new ruling establishes scored category between ordinary-autocracy baseline and in-territory active-perpetration floor
observations: UAE published 23.4 priced the entity as an ordinary mid-band autocracy with constrained civic space, migrant-labor inequity, and weak accountability — it did not price the documented, ongoing arming and financing of the RSF in Sudan, a force the UN FFM found bears the hallmarks of genocide

Four independent source chains (UN FFM Feb 2026, Conflict Insights Group June 2026, Amnesty International embargo-breach documentation, Sudan ICJ filing March 2025) confirm the UAE weapons, drones, and mercenary supply to the RSF — including the specific enablement of the October 2025 El Fasher mass atrocities

The ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY ruling pulls INT, ACC, SYS, and EMP while preserving BND (domestic scope unchanged); composite 23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0; medium-high confidence with ICJ-unadjudicated magnitude cap
Sudan's 0.0 floor is reinforced by the same evidence, never supplanted; external-sponsorship scored once under the sponsor to avoid double-counting the in-territory perpetrator's floor designation

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Technology: COMPELLED-REMEDY Hold and Boundary Watch

observations: Microsoft confirmed at 65.3 Established with positive-watch: the June 8 Azure-Israel publication (Unit 8200 access terminated, Human Rights Review Board announced) is the compelled remediation already assessed June 7 — AB2 anchor 3, max defensible +1.3 to 66.6, sub-threshold; AB5 reparation to surveilled Palestinians absent

Alphabet at 40.0 at the exact Developing/Functional boundary (40/41): no entity-specific evidence this cycle; Norway sovereign wealth fund June 4 governance proposal is institutional-investor pressure, not an executed action; EFF's June 8 call for Google to follow Microsoft's COMPELLED-REMEDY model places Project Nimbus under the same scrutiny framework

The COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling now has three corporate anchor cases (Alphabet June 3, Microsoft June 7, Microsoft June 8 confirmation) — the distinction between genuinely self-initiated course correction and pressure-driven remediation is established as a durable scoring principle

observations: UnitedHealth Group at 10.2: DOJ MA criminal/civil fraud probe, AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit (plus MultiPlan), Lifepoint June 2 federal complaint, and DOJ Antitrust MultiPlan grand-jury subpoena constitute the highest sovereign-enforcement density in the current cycle; all pre-adjudication discipline held

AZ AG cartel designation, if adjudicated, would extend ACC and EQU scoring exposure to Aetna, Cigna, Humana, BCBS Arizona, Centene, Molina Healthcare, and Elevance Health — a sector-wide repricing event with the deepest single-cycle corporate-index implications of any current open enforcement action

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Regulatory Environment: Great American AI Act and EU AI Act Timeline
sectorAlerts.headline: Great American AI Act at procedural stage; EU AI Act GPAI obligations 55 days out; Anthropic net-neutral on military-contract and voluntary-EO signals
observations: Great American AI Act (June 4–5, bipartisan): proposed mandatory safety-framework publication, twice-yearly independent audits, and $1M/day penalties for non-compliance; three-year state-preemption clause; not yet enacted — procedural/legislative stage

EU AI Act GPAI obligations effective August 2: no assessed AI lab has published required transparency documentation; fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover; 55 days from this cycle
Trump June 2 voluntary pre-release-review EO: voluntary framework, not mandatory compliance; no scored impact on any lab this cycle

Anthropic confirmed at 59.1: Mythos gating (B4/I1 boundary positive), DC Circuit Pentagon appeal ongoing with judges divided, Great American AI Act at procedural stage — net-neutral composite; OpenAI confirmed at 27.5 on same framework

observations: UN Fact-Finding Mission (Feb 19, 2026): RSF attacks in El Fasher bear the hallmarks of genocide; UN documents show UK-origin and Chinese weapons reaching the RSF via UAE-connected supply channels

Conflict Insights Group (June 2026): UAE continues supplying weapons and foreign mercenaries to the RSF, with specific documentation that UAE support enabled the October 2025 El Fasher mass atrocities targeting Zaghawa and Fur populations
Amnesty International: UAE re-exporting Chinese weaponry to the RSF in breach of the UN arms embargo, routed via Libya, Chad, and Uganda — a documented multi-country supply chain

Sudan ICJ filing (March 2025) alleges UAE complicity in genocide through extensive financial, political, and military RSF support; adjudication pending — the unadjudicated status is the operative magnitude cap for the proposed 18.4 score

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — DEI Disclosure and EEOC Title VII Watch
sectorAlerts.headline: 65% Fortune 500 DEI disclosure decline year-over-year; EEOC Title VII warning on DEI-elimination programs introduces enforcement-density signal
observations: Fortune 500 DEI disclosures declined 65% year-over-year — a structural weakening of the transparency environment relevant to EQU and EMP dimension scoring across the corporate index

EEOC Title VII warning to Fortune 500 companies on DEI-elimination programs introduces a potential enforcement channel for the EQU and ACC dimensions; no individual entity has crossed the proposal threshold this cycle

The disclosure-decline and EEOC-warning combination creates a compounding ACC-floor risk for entities where reduced disclosure coincides with active enforcement actions — monitored as index-wide drift rather than entity-specific events

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY (United Arab Emirates — new ruling; first application)
methodologyNotes.description: Documented, ongoing external-state sponsorship of another actor's genocide is scorable harm that pulls the integrity-of-care dimensions (INT, ACC, SYS, EMP) downward to reflect that the state's external conduct directly enables mass atrocity — even where the sponsoring state is not perpetrating atrocity on its own territory. The BND dimension is preserved when the domestic scope and service-delivery capacity of the sponsoring state are unchanged. The ruling produces a downgrade, not a floor designation: the UAE is a complicit external sponsor (not an in-territory perpetrator), and the ICJ case is unadjudicated (capping the magnitude). The complicity is scored once, under the sponsor, and never double-counted against the in-territory perpetrator's floor. The UAE case (23.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical, −5.0, medium-high confidence) is the first application of this ruling. It establishes a scored category between ordinary-autocracy baseline and the in-territory active-perpetration floor for future external-state-sponsorship cases.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-PERPETRATION-FLOOR-REINFORCEMENT (Russia, Sudan, Israel — re-affirmed with June 8 evidence)
methodologyNotes.description: Russia (June 1–2 largest 2026 bombardment: 656 drones and 73 missiles, 22 civilians killed, Putin rejected talks June 6), Sudan (UAE arming RSF confirmed by Conflict Insights Group and Amnesty; external-state-sponsorship scored under UAE, not against this floor), and Israel (970+ killed since October 2025 ceasefire, June 6 tent-camp strike, OHCHR ethnic-cleansing concerns) remain at the absolute 0.0 floor. New adverse June 8 evidence reinforces but cannot move the floor. The UAE external-sponsorship complicity in Sudan is scored under UAE, not as a modification to Sudan's sovereign floor.

methodologyNotes.name: PRICED / SAME-REGIME-CONTINUATION (United States, Bolivia, India — re-applied)
methodologyNotes.description: All three entities are now at their recently-applied lower scores (US 17.5 applied June 9, Bolivia 18.4 applied June 9, India 15.6 applied June 1) and the June 8 evidence constitutes same-regime continuation already priced. US: June 4 ICE death-reporting rescission and 18 in-custody deaths YTD reinforce the ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor without altering the magnitude. Bolivia: June 8 impunity-law signing and 10 killed/365 arrested are the exact events the applied downgrade encoded. India: ~5,000 Bengali-Muslim deportations deepen the EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4 profile set days earlier. Confirmed; no re-litigation.

methodologyNotes.name: COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION (Microsoft — confirmed second cycle)
methodologyNotes.description: The June 8 published Azure-Israel inquiry is confirmed as the compelled remediation already assessed June 7 — not a new or expanded action. AB2 anchor 3 (course correction under pressure) applies: the action was triggered by the August 2025 Guardian/+972 exposé, the Covington review was scope-limited by design, and the Human Rights Review Board is a prospective commitment with no observable outcomes. Maximum defensible move +1.3 (to 66.6), sub-threshold; AB5 reparation to surveilled Palestinians absent. Microsoft confirmed at 65.3 Established with positive-watch. The COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling now has three confirmed corporate-index applications (Alphabet June 3, Microsoft June 7, Microsoft June 8 confirmation) and is established as a durable scoring principle for conflict-zone tech-contract remediations.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT (DRC — re-affirmed)
methodologyNotes.description: Ebola Bundibugyo (515 cases, 91 deaths, no approved vaccine, 17 of 36 Ituri health zones) is an exogenous epidemiological shock revealing already-priced state-response incapacity absent documented deliberate obstruction. The NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT ruling holds at the near-floor 2.3 composite. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response (access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression) remains the conversion trigger. Outbreak trajectory alone — even at current acceleration — does not break the overlay.

methodologyNotes.name: ROTATION-CONFIRM plus NOT-IN-INDEX SUBSTITUTION (Luxembourg, Liechtenstein, San Jose, Pittsburgh, Unitree Robotics)
methodologyNotes.description: Five rotation entities confirmed at published scores without acute negative events. AgiBot (scanner rotation nominee) is not in any published index (not-scorable) and was substituted with Unitree Robotics (robotics rank 43), which reconstructs EXACT at 35.9. Luxembourg's validate-indexes warning (81.3 vs reconstruct 83.5, diff 2.2) is a known pre-existing top-band cohort baseline anomaly shared with Iceland, Finland, Denmark, Sweden, and Norway — not a new math-hygiene flag; no proposal. AgiBot logged as not-in-index; recommend adding to robotics-labs for future scorability.

forwardTriggers.trigger: EU Returns Regulation enters force. Documented EU-facilitated return without individual assessment where a third-country national faces persecution constitutes a scored signal. India at 15.6 is the primary non-refoulement watch entity. Confirmation of 145+ state-attributable enforced disappearances among deportees is a secondary India trigger.

WARN Act last-working-day sign-or-forfeit window closes for approximately 30,000 affected workers. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in the NJ state proceedings or Washington class action constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from 0.6 points above Critical.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July with 17-health-zone spread and no approved vaccine. Documented deliberate state obstruction of WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite.

Phase-2 MDL deadline. Merits adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit. A modeled downgrade of −2.3 would cross from 0.3 points above Critical into Critical.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect. Fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. No assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation.

ICJ ruling in Sudan v. UAE (filed March 2025 alleging UAE complicity in genocide). Verified cessation of RSF arms transfers is the primary reversal signal for the proposed 18.4 Critical composite. Documented direct UAE-force participation in Sudan would move toward a floor assessment.

Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal. Further military deployments with documented casualties under the signed law reinforce the applied 18.4 Critical baseline.

Appellate review of Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded June 4 ICE post-release death-reporting requirement is the first upward-direction signal within the applied 17.5 Critical baseline.

Verified Human Rights Review Board execution that demonstrably blocks or exits a harmful national-security contract — conversion trigger from the current positive-watch to a scorable upgrade within Established. Quiet reinstatement of terminated Unit 8200 access is a negative trigger.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the four converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit, Lifepoint federal complaint, or DOJ Antitrust MultiPlan grand-jury subpoena.

DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth. Favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established boundary crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement. Enacted Great American AI Act compliance documentation would be independently scorable.

Confirmation of 145+ state-attributable enforced disappearances among deportees at scale — a new harm category independently scorable beyond the priced 15.6 baseline. Judicial findings on wrongful citizen expulsion are a secondary directional indicator.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If Sudan's ICJ case finds UAE liable for complicity in the RSF genocide, does the adjudicated finding remove the magnitude cap currently holding the proposed composite at 18.4 — or does the ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY ruling hold a structural floor above 0.0 for external sponsors who lack in-territory perpetration?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: The proposed UAE 18.4 rests on the ICJ case being unadjudicated — adjudication finding liability could either lift the cap or confirm the structural distinction between external sponsor and in-territory perpetrator.
